survivors_flagged.one_line: Diamba Sud (Senegal) ESIA approved; mining permit + final construction decision targeted mid-2026, but a project-development/gold-price bet rather than mispriced special-sit asymmetry.
Ancora (May-2026) demanding H.B. Fuller abandon AMS deal and run a full strategic review incl. sale; live activist + dated AMS trigger.
Live breakup merger-arb: shareholders approved 6/23/26, fintech sold to Smith Ventures + bank combines w/ CommerceOne, $14.23-19.18/sh, close Q3 2026 pending Fed + AL/UT state-banking approvals.
Value-crystallization sleeve: DayOne spin (planned ~$20B US IPO, GDS holds stake) + 2nd C-REIT asset drop-down expected to close 2H 2026 monetizing the suppressed underlying.
Dated ICE Detention Reengineering Initiative milestone (new model / 34 facilities by Sept 30 2026) + pre-qualified vendor; real dated driver but contract-flow/thematic, not a discrete binary.
Active strategic-alternatives breakup: ONCAP AMES JV (cash + 2nd-lien + 43% stake) closing end-June 2026, AMES Australia process by year-end, UK liquidating — becomes pure-play NA building products.
FLAMINGO-01 Phase 3 (GLSI-100/GP2) HLA-A*02 randomized interim pending; microcap dated binary with strong open-label signal and large asymmetry.
Genmab acquiring Merus via $8B all-cash tender ($97/sh) closing early Q1 2026, plus petosemtamab Phase 3 head-and-neck interim readout dated in 2026.
Pilot Mountain tungsten PFS results dated June 30 2026 + July 1 investor call, DPA-funded ($6.2M) US defense-metal-independence supply story; hard dated milestone but speculative microcap.
Announced Feb 17 2026 tax-free separation of NAPA (auto) from Motion (industrial) into two public cos, targeted Q1 2027 completion, investor days H2 2026 — clean dated spin-off, not yet effected.

Structure Therapeutics: best-in-class oral GLP-1 (16.3% wt-loss), positive end-of-Ph2 FDA feedback, Phase 3 registrational start Q3 2026, $1.5B cash to 2028 — dated binary + prime big-pharma oral-obesity takeout target.

GeoPark: Parex nominated 6 directors for July 14 2026 AGM (vote deadline July 13) — live proxy contest; Parex's $9 takeover ended Dec'25 but strategic-alternatives overhang persists, dated hardening trigger ~2wks out.

FDA PMA under review + pending CMS/Medicare Galleri reimbursement decision is a live binary, but the Feb-2026 NHS trial missing its primary endpoint (Stage III/IV reduction) badly damages the asymmetry; Samsung $110M raise extends runway. One real catalyst, muddied.

Definitive Amazon merger ($90 cash/0.3210 AMZN, ~58% written-consent approval) is a real arb, but expected close is 2027 and conditioned on antitrust/FDI/telecom approvals PLUS HIBLEO-4 satellite-replacement milestones; long-dated with completion risk.

Clean merger-arb: Baker Hughes $210/sh all-cash, shareholders already approved, EC Form CO filed May-2026 with remedy talks underway, both sides reaffirm a July-2026 target close; imminent, dated, >=2 gates.

verified.catalyst: Fortuna Mining's Diamba Sud (Senegal) gold project cleared ESIA (decree 15 Jun 2026) and awaits an exploitation permit plus a final construction decision targeted mid-2026.
verified.dated_milestone: Feasibility study completion end-Q2 2026 and final construction decision shortly after (mid-2026); exploitation permit pending (applied 4 Feb 2026); first gold pour Q2 2028.
verified.bloom_gates: none cleanly — G1 no named counterparty (no offtake/JV/acquirer); G2 the permit filing is the company's own, not a third-party commitment; G3 the $563M PEA NPV is published, not an unpriced figure
verified.edge: L — PEA NPV5 $563M / IRR 72% is explicitly struck at $2,750/oz gold, so the asymmetry is leveraged gold-price exposure, not a mispricing; headline ESIA catalyst already fired 15 Jun 2026 and economics public since Oct 2025
verified.primary_source: Fortuna Mining "obtains key environmental approval for Diamba Sud" (Mining Weekly / company release, 15 Jun 2026); Exploitation Permit Application release (GlobeNewswire, 10 Feb 2026); Diamba Sud PEA (Fortuna, Oct 2025)
verified.analysis: The named triggering event (Diamba Sud ESIA decree) already fired 15 Jun 2026, and the project economics have been public since the October 2025 PEA, so there is no currently-unpriced figure. The remaining milestones — exploitation permit and a final construction decision in mid-2026 — are routine self-filed development gates with no named counterparty, offtake, JV, or acquirer, and the NPV/IRR are explicitly modeled on a $2,750/oz gold deck, making the upside a leveraged gold-price bet rather than a mispriced special-situation asymmetry. Fails forward/unpriced and the not-macro test; matches the triage's own characterization.

verified.catalyst: Ancora's demand that H.B. Fuller drop the AMS acquisition and run a strategic review/sale was rejected — FUL launched a formal recommended GBP715M scheme to buy AMS on June 25, 2026, the opposite of the activist thesis.
verified.dated_milestone: No near-term dated forward trigger; Ancora only threatens a proxy fight "next year" (FY2027 annual meeting, undated). AMS scheme completion expected end-2026 but that is the deal proceeding, not an activist value-unlock.
verified.bloom_gates: G1 named counterparty (Ancora vs. FUL board) is satisfied, but the dated trigger (UK Takeover Panel June 18 / formal offer) has FIRED and the board rejected the activist's core demand. No G2 concrete forward commitment and no G3 specific currently-unpriced figure remain.
verified.edge: L — the dated AMS trigger has fired (formal recommended scheme launched Jun 25), board rejected the drop-deal/sale demand, and the only remaining activist catalyst is an undated "proxy fight next year"; stock is re-rating down (~-5% today) on the deal proceeding, not pricing a sale premium.
verified.primary_source: FUL formal AMS offer announcement / Ancora response, June 25, 2026 (Investing.com "H.B. Fuller Q2 2026 slides: GBP715M medical deal"; Ancora opposition letter, businesswire.com 2026-05-26)
verified.analysis: Ancora (May 2026) wanted FUL to abandon AMS and pursue a full strategic review including a sale, but on June 25, 2026 FUL did the opposite — it announced a formal recommended GBP715M cash scheme to acquire AMS, defying the activist. The dated catalyst the triage flagged (the UK Takeover Panel June 18 put-up-or-shut-up window) has fired against the thesis, the board is recommending the deal rather than a sale, and Ancora's only remaining lever is a vaguely-dated "proxy fight next year." With no concrete near-term dated forward milestone and the activist's value-unlock effectively rejected, this is a fired/macro-resolved situation, not a live unpriced forward catalyst.

verified.catalyst: Late-stage break-up merger-arb: shareholders approved 6/23/26 the CommerceOne acquisition of Green Dot Bank plus Smith Ventures' take-private of the fintech for $8.11 cash + 0.2215 CommerceOne shares (~$14.23), with only bank-regulatory clearance left before a Q3 2026 close.
verified.dated_milestone: Bank-regulatory approvals (Federal Reserve + Alabama State Banking Dept + Utah Dept of Financial Institutions) gating an expected Q3 2026 deal close; shareholder vote already passed 6/23/26 (99% of votes, ~72% of shares out).
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (named counterparties CommerceOne Financial + Smith Ventures), G2 (fixed contractual consideration $8.11 cash + 0.2215 shares), G3 (specific unpriced spread: ~$13.34 live vs ~$14.23 deal value)
verified.primary_source: Green Dot IR press release / SEC 8-K, "Green Dot and CommerceOne Shareholders Approve Pending Acquisition," June 23, 2026 (ir.greendot.com); confirmed by Yahoo Finance same date
verified.kill_fact: Every load-bearing fact independently confirmed against primary/secondary sources. DEFM14A / Form 425 / businesswire (11/23/2025 announcement): each GDOT share converts to EXACTLY $8.11 cash + 0.2215 New CommerceOne shares. StockTitan/IR/Yahoo (6/23/2026): GDOT + CommerceOne shareholders approved — 99% of GDOT shares voted in favor (~72% of outstanding); only Fed + Alabama State Banking Dept + Utah Dept of Financial Institutions clearance remains, Q3 2026 close. Live FMP quote $13.34 (matches LINE), 52-wk low $9.31 (matches dossier floor). This is a real, dated, forward, non-macro, binding regulatory catalyst with vote risk retired — clears all three Bloom gates. One interpretive correction (not a kill): the ~$14.23 deal value is the LOW END of an officially stated $14.23–$19.18 consideration range that floats with the tangible-book multiple on the unlisted New CommerceOne entity, so the stock leg's realized payout is genuinely uncertain; using $14.23 is the conservative/correct arb anchor. Spread is thin (~6.7%) with ~$0.87 upside vs ~$1.56 downside to the $11.80 break floor — defined-risk clip-into-close arb, not a wide mispricing. Score 6 (capped) appropriate.
verified.analysis: Independently confirmed via Green Dot IR / 8-K / Yahoo (6/23/26): Green Dot and CommerceOne shareholders approved the strategic separation; CommerceOne acquires Green Dot Bank into a new public bank holding company while Smith Ventures takes the non-bank fintech private, each GDOT share converting to $8.11 cash + 0.2215 CommerceOne shares (~$14.23). Only Fed + Alabama + Utah bank-regulatory clearance remains, dated to Q3 2026 close — a real, dated, forward, non-macro catalyst that clears all three Bloom gates. Caveat: the spread has compressed to ~6-7% (live $13.34 vs deal ~$14.23) and the dossier's own asymmetry shows ~$0.87 upside vs ~$1.56 downside to the $11.80 break floor, so this is a clean defined-risk arb to clip into close, not a wide mispricing — hence a capped score.

verified.catalyst: GDS's ~19.9% stake in DayOne Data Centers (worth $2.2B+, vs GDS's own $5.6B mkt cap) crystallizes via DayOne's planned ~$20B US IPO, while a second data-center asset drop-down to its Shanghai C-REIT is set to monetize more suppressed underlying value in 2H 2026.
verified.dated_milestone: DayOne confidential SEC draft registration filed ~mid-March 2026 (Bloomberg); US listing targeted ~late 2026; second C-REIT asset drop-down expected to close 2H 2026 (per GDS CFO). Next date: DayOne public F-1/roadmap and 2H26 C-REIT drop-down close.
verified.instrument: GDS Holdings ADR (NASDAQ: GDS); HKEX 9698 dual-listed
verified.bloom_gates: G1+G2+G3: G1 named counterparty (DayOne IPO underwriters JPMorgan/MS/BofA/Citi; Shanghai SE C-REIT fund 508060). G2 concrete commitment (CFO guided a 2nd C-REIT asset sale to close 2H 2026; DayOne board pursuing US IPO with confidential filing already lodged). G3 specific unpriced figure ($2.2B+ DayOne stake vs $5.6B GDS market cap; first C-REIT drop-down recycled RMB2.1bn net at a 20% retained stake).
verified.edge: M — both legs real/dated/forward and the SoP discount is concrete (DayOne stake alone ~40% of GDS market cap before a $20B IPO mark-up, stock at 52-wk lows), but timing is uncertain: DayOne IPO is confidential/late-2026 and IPO windows slip, and the C-REIT drop-down is a recurring/known mechanism (partly anticipated), so it is more re-rating optionality than a hard binary date.
verified.primary_source: Bloomberg via Mingtiandi/MENA Fintech, "DayOne near confidential US IPO filing" (~17 Mar 2026) and GDS Q1 2026 earnings release (DayOne stake >$2.2B); GDS C-REIT IPO completion + CFO 2H26 second drop-down guidance (GDS investor releases, Jul–Aug 2025).
verified.analysis: GDS trades at ~$28.76 (mkt cap $5.6B), near its 52-week low ($26.97) and far below its 50/200-day averages (~$38), while its ~19.9% DayOne stake is independently valued above $2.2B (implying DayOne ~$11B+, pre the targeted ~$20B IPO) — a clear suppressed sum-of-parts. The DayOne US IPO has already reached confidential SEC filing (~mid-March 2026, per Bloomberg) with bulge-bracket underwriters, and the CFO has guided a second data-center asset drop-down into the Shanghai C-REIT (fund 508060) to close in 2H 2026, repeating the 2025 monetization that netted ~RMB2.1bn. Both catalysts are forward and unfired, but neither has a hard fixed date and the C-REIT mechanism is partly anticipated, so this is a WATCH-tier re-rating thesis rather than an actionable hard event.

verified.catalyst: ICE's "Detention Reengineering Initiative" targets a new detention model across 34 owned facilities by Sept 30 2026, with GEO in non-binding talks to sell/manage some sites — but the warehouse plan is paused and GEO was skipped on the actual awards.
verified.dated_milestone: ICE program dates: new model by Sept 30 2026, all facilities activated by Nov 30 2026 — but the warehouse conversion was paused (Zoley, May 2026); no GEO-specific contract date exists.
verified.bloom_gates: None pass. G1 fails: ICE is the named counterparty but is in non-binding "subject to price" talks and bypassed GEO/CoreCivic, awarding the $400M warehouse contracts to GardaWorld ($313M) and KVG ($113M). G2 fails: no concrete GEO commitment/signed contract. G3 fails: the only concrete figure is broad FY26 guidance ($2.95-3.10B, raised), already priced.
verified.edge: L — thesis is a macro/policy contract-flow bet that is already substantially re-rated (stock +80% post-election, FY26 guidance raised, near 52-wk high $32 vs $12.5 low, analyst PTs $29.50-$40); the specific warehouse driver is paused and GEO lost the primary awards.
verified.primary_source: Bisnow, "ICE Awards $400M In Warehouse Contracts, Bypasses Detention Center Stalwarts" (GEO/CoreCivic skipped); Axios "ICE targets Plan B" + GEO Q1 call (Zoley: warehouse project paused), May 2026; American Immigration Council blog on the 34-facility model timeline (Sept 30/Nov 30 2026), 2026.
verified.analysis: The ICE Detention Reengineering Initiative is real and dated (Sept 30 / Nov 30 2026), but it is a government-program timeline, not a GEO-specific discrete binary — and as of May 2026 the warehouse conversion was paused and ICE awarded the major warehouse contracts to non-incumbent vendors (GardaWorld $313M, KVG $113M), skipping GEO. GEO's only linkage is non-binding "potential sale subject to mutual agreement on price" talk, which is neither a named commitment nor an unpriced figure; the broad ICE-expansion thesis is already reflected in the +80% post-election move, raised FY26 guidance, and a near-52-week-high price. This is a thematic policy/contract-flow macro bet that has largely fired and re-rated, failing all three Bloom gates.

verified.catalyst: Griffon's pure-play-NA-building-products breakup is largely executed; the only forward leg left is closing the signed AMES Australasia JV ($185M cash + $50M note, 49% retained) by FYE Sept 2026.

verified.bloom_gates: G1 (named counterparty: Hupfeld-led mgmt group + Australian investors for Australasia; ONCAP/Venanpri for US-Canada) and G2 (definitive agreement, concrete $185M+$50M terms). G3 fails — proceeds disclosed and largely priced.
verified.edge: L — the transformative legs are already fired (US/Canada JV closed 2026-06-10; UK liquidating since Q2); stock at $94.21 sits near 52wk high $97.70, far above 200d $80.33, so the pure-play re-rate from ~$65-78 has already happened.
verified.primary_source: Griffon 8-K / Businesswire "Taking Strategic Actions to Maximize Shareholder Value" (2026-02-04); Veritage JV close PR (2026-06-10); Investing.com AMES Australasia $235M JV (2026-06-08); FMP quote GFF $94.21 (2026-06-29).
verified.analysis: The flagged catalyst is mostly already FIRED, and the triage note is stale: the ONCAP/Venanpri AMES US-Canada JV (Veritage Brands) closed 2026-06-10 (not "closing end-June"), AMES UK ceased operations 2026-03-31 and is in liquidation, leaving only the signed AMES Australasia JV to close by FYE Sept 2026. That remaining leg is a definitive agreement with fully disclosed terms ($185M cash + $50M sub note, 49% retained), so it is a residual closing event rather than an unpriced forward catalyst, and GFF at $94.21 trades near its 52-week high ($97.70) versus the $65 low and $80 200-day, evidencing the pure-play re-rate is substantially complete. Gates G1/G2 pass on the structural facts, but G3 fails (no specific currently-unpriced figure) and the edge is low — this is WATCH for the FYE-Sept Australasia close and any post-close capital-return announcement, not an ACTIVE entry.

verified.catalyst: FLAMINGO-01 Phase 3 randomized HLA-A*02 interim (superiority/futility at 14 of 28 IBCFS events) in GLSI-100/GP2 HER2+ breast cancer.
verified.dated_milestone: Event-driven interim at 14 events — NO company calendar date; randomized arm still enrolling as of Dec 22, 2025, company expanding HLA-A*02 arms to avoid stopping before interim. Next dated touchpoint = none firm (interim likely 2027+).
verified.bloom_gates: None cleanly met — the binary is real but G3 fails: the interim figure (HR 0.3 / 70% reduction design) is specific but the readout is UNDATED and not forward-imminent; no G1 counterparty / no G2 dated commitment.
verified.edge: M — genuine large-asymmetry microcap binary with a strong (80% recurrence reduction) open-label signal, but that signal is the non-randomized arm and already public; the randomized confirmatory interim has no clock.
verified.primary_source: Greenwich LifeSciences PR "Additional Updates on FLAMINGO-01 and Corporate Strategy," Dec 22, 2025 (interim design, randomized arm still enrolling, no interim date); open-label 80% signal Dec 2025, re-presented ASCO June 1, 2026.
verified.analysis: The verifiable catalyst is a real Phase 3 binary, but the randomized HLA-A*02 interim is event-driven (14 of 28 events) with no company-provided date; as of Dec 22, 2025 the randomized arm was still enrolling and the company is deliberately enlarging the arms so enrollment does not stop before any interim, putting a confirmatory readout years out and undated. The headline 80% recurrence-reduction figure comes from the non-randomized open-label arm and has been public since Dec 2025 and re-presented at AACR and ASCO (June 1, 2026), so it is already fired/re-rated rather than a forward unpriced figure. Fails STRICT gating on the DATED requirement: no specific next-date milestone exists, so this is WATCH-grade thesis at best but does not clear the catalyst bar today.

verified.primary_source: Genmab/Merus IR press release "Completion of Tender Offer" (deal closed 2025-12-12) + Genmab IR acquisition release on petosemtamab Phase 3 "anticipated in 2026" / launch 2027 (Sept 2025)
verified.analysis: The triage premise is stale and incorrect: the $8B all-cash Merus tender at $97/share did not close "early Q1 2026" — it CLOSED December 12, 2025, so the merger-arb lane is fired with zero remaining spread; GMAB is the acquirer, not an arb target. The only forward catalyst is the acquired petosemtamab Phase 3 head-and-neck program (LiGeR-HN1/HN2), but Genmab guides topline only "anticipated in 2026" with no quarter and a 2027 launch — failing the strict DATED test, and as a large-cap pharma pipeline bet it is not a specific currently-unpriced figure. No gate clears, so tier NONE / score 3.

verified.lane: supply-shortage + dated binary (US tungsten defense-metal independence)

verified.dated_milestone: PFS results release 2026-06-30; live investor call 2026-07-01 11:00 ET via 6ix (next date: 2026-06-30)
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (named counterparty: US DoD / Dept of War, $6.2M DPA Title III award July 2025), G2 (concrete commitment: funded PFS deliverable on a fixed date), G3 (specific currently-unpriced figure: PFS NPV/IRR/capex/production not yet disclosed, arriving 6/30)
verified.edge: M — hard dated forward milestone with a real un-priced figure (PFS economics) and stock has de-rated ~34% off highs to $14.58 (below 50/200-day MAs ~$16.4/$16.7), so the catalyst is not pre-priced; but it is a speculative ~$2.4B-mcap single-asset binary where weak PFS economics (low APT-price assumption, high capex) could disappoint, capping conviction.
verified.primary_source: StockTitan / Guardian Metal RNS "Pilot Mountain PFS Update & Investor Presentation" (announced 2026-06-26); DoD award war.gov + PR Newswire (2025-07-23)
verified.kill_fact: Pilot Mountain PFS publication CONFIRMED for 2026-06-30 with a 2026-07-01 11:00 ET investor call, backed by the $6.2M US DoD DPA Title III award to subsidiary Golden Metal Resources (USA) LLC (StockTitan/AccessNewswire RNS). Genuine dated forward single-asset binary, not yet fired as of 06-29, not a macro bet; PFS by definition produces first-ever NPV/IRR/capex (announcement does not pre-disclose them). CORRECTION: the cited '12.53Mt @ 0.27% WO3 / 34.3kt' is the STALE 2017/18 historical estimate; the current S-K 1300 MRE is materially smaller (~indicated 8.69Mt @ 0.206% + inferred 1.78Mt @ 0.169% WO3, ~10.5Mt total at lower grade). Also note the separate Aug-2026 date floating in coverage is the mine Plan of Operations/permitting submission, NOT the PFS. Thesis intact: thin microcap, downside is a disappointing study (capex/grade/APT) — size as speculative event trade.
verified.analysis: Verified via StockTitan/company RNS (announced 2026-06-26) and war.gov/PR Newswire: GMTL will publish the Pilot Mountain tungsten PFS on 2026-06-30 with a 2026-07-01 investor call, backed by a confirmed $6.2M US DoD DPA Title III award and a 12.53Mt @ 0.27% WO3 / 34.3kt resource. This is a genuine dated, forward, single-asset binary — not yet fired and not a macro bet — with the first-ever PFS economics as the unpriced figure. Downside risk is a disappointing study (capex/grade/APT assumptions) in a thin microcap, so size as a speculative event trade rather than a high-conviction position.

Price $115.68 (~matches $115.74), below 200-day ($120.77). Catalyst is forward (closes Q1 2027), not fired. CORRECTION: line's '2026 EPS guidance was cut' risk is timing-confused — at Q1 (Apr 21) GPC MAINTAINED guidance ($7.50-8.00 adj EPS); the cut came LATER at Q2 (to ~$7.75 midpoint), post-dating the April reaffirmation the line cites. Also note initial 2026 guide was already below ~$8.41 consensus. Skeptic flag: heavily-covered Dividend King, SoP already partly consensus (RJ upgrade Feb 24), discount only ~modestly un-priced.
verified.analysis: GPC announced a board-approved, tax-free separation of NAPA (auto, ~$15B/$1.2B EBITDA) and Motion (industrial, ~$9B/$1.1B+ EBITDA) on Feb 17 2026, reaffirmed on track for Q1 2027 at April Q1 earnings, with J.P. Morgan/Guggenheim engaged and Elliott's >$1B stake forcing the value-unlock. At $115.74 the stock sits below its 200-day average and ~25% under Raymond James' $145 SoP target (Motion ~15x fwd EBITDA vs peer Applied Industrial ~17x), so the spin discount is largely un-priced and the catalyst is forward, not fired. Next dated catalyst is the H2-2026 investor days that set standalone segment targets; risk is that this is a heavily-covered Dividend King where the SoP narrative is already partly in consensus and 2026 EPS guidance was cut.

verified.analysis: The headline catalysts the triage flagged — best-in-class oral GLP-1 efficacy and positive end-of-Phase 2 FDA feedback — are already fired: the FDA EOP2 meeting is completed (CMO confirmed Phase 3 will use a 2.5mg starting dose "based on our End of Phase 2 meeting with the FDA," per the June 5 2026 release) and the ~$2.9B market cap reflects a ~3x re-rate off the lows. What remains forward is the Q3 2026 Phase 3 initiation, but that is a process milestone with the actual registrational data 1-2+ years out, and the "prime big-pharma takeout" thesis has no named counterparty or concrete commitment (Bloom G1/G3 fail). It is a legitimate, well-funded clinical story to monitor, but not a dated, currently-unpriced forward binary — hence WATCH, not ACTIVE.

verified.primary_source: Parex Resources Schedule 13D/A Amendment No. 4 on GeoPark Ltd, filed/accepted 2026-04-08 / 2026-04-09 (via StockTitan SEC filing summary); corroborated by Parex GlobeNewswire release 2026-04-09
verified.analysis: The triage thesis is stale and inverted. Parex Resources filed 13D/A Amendment No. 4 on April 8, 2026 (SEC-accepted Apr 9) withdrawing its prior nomination of six independent directors for GeoPark's 2026 AGM and terminating the related nomination/indemnification agreements, explicitly stating it no longer intends to solicit proxies; the withdrawal followed GeoPark's March 5, 2026 share issuance that diluted Parex from 11.8% to 9.4%. With the $9.00/share takeover dead since Dec 2025 (Parex refused to raise) and the slate pulled, there is no live, dated, forward proxy/M&A catalyst into the July AGM — only a residual passive stake and a generic strategic-alternatives overhang, which does not pass the gate.

verified.catalyst: FDA decision on the Galleri multi-cancer early detection test PMA (final module submitted Jan 29, 2026; under review) is a live approve/reject binary for the company's core asset.
verified.dated_milestone: FDA PMA decision expected mid-to-late 2026 (analyst-estimated; no firm FDA-published date). NHS-Galleri primary-endpoint miss already disclosed Feb 20, 2026; Samsung $110M raise closed Jun 25, 2026.
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (named counterparty = FDA, specific PMA under active review). Fails G3 — no specific currently-unpriced figure; the unpriced Stage III/IV efficacy claim was the NHS endpoint, which already missed and re-rated.
verified.primary_source: GRAIL/PRNewswire PMA-submission release (Jan 29, 2026) + MedTech Dive / GenomeWeb NHS-Galleri endpoint-miss coverage (Feb 20, 2026) + GRAIL Samsung $110M financing release (Jun 25, 2026)
verified.analysis: GRAIL's Galleri PMA is genuinely under active FDA review (final module submitted Jan 29, 2026) — a real, forward, named-counterparty binary on the company's sole material asset, supporting WATCH not NONE. But the catalyst is materially muddied: the Feb 20, 2026 NHS-Galleri trial missed its primary endpoint (no significant Stage III/IV reduction over 3 years, ~-51% reaction), so the strongest unpriced efficacy thesis has already fired against the bull case, and management's claim that the miss won't sway FDA is unverified. The CMS coverage category exists but eligibility only phases in from 2029, and the FDA decision date itself is analyst-estimated (mid-to-late 2026) rather than FDA-published — leaving genuine but soft, un-firmly-dated optionality.

verified.catalyst: Definitive Amazon takeout of Globalstar at $90.00 cash/share (or 0.3210 AMZN capped at $90), ~58% already approved by written consent, expected to close in 2027.
verified.dated_milestone: Merger close expected in 2027; outside date ~April 2027 (extendable into 2028) pending antitrust/FDI/telecom approvals + HIBLEO-4 replacement-satellite milestones.
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (named counterparty: Amazon, definitive agreement ~Apr 2026); G2 (concrete commitment: $90 cash/0.3210 AMZN, ~58% written-consent approval); G3 (specific unpriced figure: ~11.5% gross spread, $80.68 vs $90)
verified.edge: M — real definitive arb with majority consent locked, but ~11.5% gross spread is the market pricing genuine completion risk over a long-dated (2027, extendable to 2028) horizon; annualized IRR is thin and exposed to antitrust/FDI/telecom and HIBLEO-4/Apple-milestone risk (up to $110M downward price adjustment).
verified.primary_source: Globalstar IR press release "Amazon to Acquire Globalstar" + GSAT 8-K/10-Q (deal announced ~2026-04-14); FMP quote GSAT $80.68 (2026-06-29)
verified.analysis: Verified, real, and specific: Amazon signed a definitive agreement (~April 2026) to acquire Globalstar at $90.00 cash/share or 0.3210 AMZN (capped at $90, ref VWAP $280.38), ~$11B, with holders of ~58% of voting power already approving by written consent. GSAT trades $80.68, a ~11.5% gross spread to deal value — forward (not fired; written consent precedes close) and not re-rated, satisfying G1/G2/G3. But it is a long-dated, conditioned arb: close expected in 2027 (outside date ~April 2027, extendable to 2028), hinging on antitrust/FDI/satellite/telecom approvals plus HIBLEO-4 replacement-satellite and Apple service-continuity milestones, with up to $110M downward consideration adjustment — so the wide spread reflects genuine completion/timeline risk, keeping it WATCH not ACTIVE.

verified.catalyst: Baker Hughes' $210/share all-cash acquisition of Chart Industries closing on the reaffirmed July 2026 target, pending EC Phase I remedy acceptance.
verified.dated_milestone: EC Phase I remedy decision deadline 10-Jul-2026; both sides reaffirm a July-2026 target close.
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (named counterparty Baker Hughes/BKR) + G2 (signed definitive agreement, shareholder-approved, Form CO filed 21-May-2026, remedies submitted)
verified.edge: L — spread is essentially closed: GTLS $208.92 vs $210 cash = ~$1.08 gross (~0.52% absolute) with close ~2 weeks out; residual return is thin and the only remaining risk is EC remedy acceptance / Phase-I extension. Fails G3 (no specific currently-unpriced figure beyond the de minimis residual spread).
verified.primary_source: TradingView/StockTitan 8-K (Chart files EC Form CO 21-May-2026, expects July-2026 close) + offshore-technology.com (23-Jun-2026, EC remedy deadline 10-Jul-2026); GTLS quote $208.92 (FMP, 29-Jun-2026).
verified.analysis: The merger-arb is fully real, dated, and forward: Baker Hughes is buying Chart at $210/share all-cash (signed 28-Jul-2025), shareholders approved, Form CO was filed with the EC on 21-May-2026, remedy talks are underway, and both sides reaffirm a July-2026 close with a 10-Jul-2026 EC Phase I deadline. However GTLS already trades at $208.92, leaving only ~0.5% to deal terms — the catalyst is overwhelmingly re-rated into the price, so there is no meaningful unpriced figure to capture (G3 not met). Real and imminent but the edge is gone; WATCH for any remedy-driven wobble that re-opens the spread, not an ACTIVE position.
